rows: Avalon Hotel and Bungalows Palm Springs
Lots of chatter about how quiet it is, but we disagree. Road noise could also hear our neighbors next door at Times great restaurant.

Dear James,    Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. While many guests find the property peaceful, we understand that perceptions of noise can vary, and we’re sorry to hear that road noise and nearby activity impacted your experience.    As a historic property located close to downtown, some ambient sound can occasionally be present, and your comments regarding sound from neighboring rooms and surrounding areas have been shared with our team for review. Guest comfort is very important to us, and we appreciate you bringing this to our attention.    Thank you again for your feedback.    Warm regards,  Cody Elliott  Rooms & Wellness Manager

New restaurant management is terrible  - New Year's Eve celebration, so fun in past years, was a disaster. Multiple complaints from everyone at the table, but absolutely no response from management. Difficult to believe a Palm Springs institution has fallen so far.
It’s a great historic property but breakfast service on the veranda could be improved

Dear Hilary,    Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re glad to hear you appreciated the charm of our historic property. We also appreciate your comments regarding breakfast service on the veranda — feedback like this is helpful as we continue to refine both our service flow and guest experience.    Thank you again for taking the time to share your thoughts, and we hope to welcome you back again soon.    Warm regards,  Cody Elliott  Rooms & Wellness Manager

Don't fall for the "estate" marketing. This is still the old Ingleside Inn at higher prices yet the same product and perhaps worse service. They've not invested in upgrading rooms (see awful room chairs and mattress)  or upgrading the pool yet are asking truly ridiculous rates when compared to the 30+ other historic hotels in the city, this one falls near the bottom.

Dear J Larsen,    Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations and that you felt disappointed with certain aspects of your stay.    Ingleside Estate is a historic property, and while many guests appreciate its preserved character and sense of place, we understand that expectations around design, comfort, and value can vary. Your comments regarding room furnishings, pool experience, and overall value have been shared with our leadership team as part of our ongoing evaluation of guest experience and future improvements.    We appreciate you offering your perspective and thank you for staying with us.    Warm regards,  Cody Elliott  Rooms & Wellness Manager

Yes close to downtown, but sometimes kind staff , and being short staffed is not just doesn’t work, there are many problems with this place including very out of date, have stay in so many motels that have been amazing, this doesn’t even qualify for decent. Probably the loudest motel we have stayed in, you can feel and hear the person in next completely different room walking. Bed sounds like it’s going to fall apart just getting on it . Doors slamming at night as people come and go . Again very nice staff but nothing is either being done with the complaints, that I see others mentioned or no one cares

Dear Tim,    Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. While we appreciate your kind words about our staff, we’re truly sorry to hear that noise, room condition, and overall comfort negatively impacted your stay. That’s certainly not the experience we want our guests to have.    As a historic property, sound can sometimes travel more than expected, but we understand how disruptive this can be and regret that it affected your rest. Your comments regarding room furnishings, overnight noise, and service consistency have been shared with our management team for review, as guest comfort and follow-through are extremely important to us.    We appreciate your candid feedback and thank you for bringing these concerns to our attention.    Warm regards,  Cody Elliott  Rooms & Wellness Manager

The stay could have been great, but the walls are very thin. Could hear restaurant adjacent to hotel cleaning outside  late into the night. No one at the front desk to assist with noise. Could hear the guest above the room snoring. Beautiful place, but sadly disappointed.

Dear Efrain,    Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. While we’re glad to hear you found the property beautiful, we’re very sorry that noise issues impacted your stay and left you disappointed. We understand how disruptive it can be to experience unwanted noise, especially late at night, and we regret that assistance was not readily available when you needed it.    As a historic property, sound can occasionally travel more than expected, and your comments regarding overnight activity and service coverage have been shared with our management team for review. Guest comfort and restful stays are extremely important to us, and we appreciate you bringing this to our attention.    Thank you again for your candid feedback.    Warm regards,  Cody Elliott  Rooms & Wellness Manager

We stayed here because it is part of the Chase “edit” hotels program. As part of the benefits we were upgraded to a deluxe room. The room was small and clean for the most part, the bed comfortable.    The food and beverage service here, was the worst I’ve experienced in a while. 3pm at the pool and there were only about 10 other people. The blonde employee would come and leave, cleaning the area, saying she would be right back to take our order. After 30 minutes we gave up, and I had wine delivered from BevMo.    The next morning at breakfast, we were simply ignored for 15 minutes. Watching the server come and go getting things for the other tables. He finally comes over and takes our order, then drops the food off when it was done and brought us our check. Never gave us ice water or asked anything, although all the other tables had ice water and he kept bringing them sauces or whatever they asked for. I finally got up and got my own water from the dispenser from the lobby.    My final bill had $100 pet fee, even though I specified at check-in he was a service animal, and would have happily supplied his airline service animal paperwork that they even printed for me.    I would recommend staying elsewhere and wouldn’t stay here again.

Dear Sarah,    Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. While we’re glad to hear that you found the room clean and comfortable, we’re truly sorry to learn about the service concerns you experienced during your stay. That is certainly not the level of attentiveness or consistency we strive to provide, particularly in our food and beverage outlets.    Your comments regarding poolside and breakfast service have been shared with our management team so we can address the gaps you described and ensure better communication and follow-through moving forward. We also regret the confusion surrounding the pet fee on your final bill. Service animals are, of course, accommodated in accordance with policy, and we understand how frustrating it must have been to see that charge appear.    We appreciate you bringing these matters to our attention and hope you might consider giving us another opportunity to provide a more seamless and positive experience in the future.    Warm regards,

We stayed here because it is part of the Chase “edit” hotels program. As part of the benefits we were upgraded to a deluxe room. The room was small and clean for the most part, the bed comfortable.     The food and beverage service here, was the worst I’ve experienced in a while. 3pm at the pool and there were only about 10 other people. The blonde employee would come and leave, cleaning the area, saying she would be right back to take our order. After 30 minutes we gave up, and I had wine delivered from BevMo.    The next morning at breakfast, we were simply ignored for 15 minutes. Watching the server come and go getting things for the other tables. He finally comes over and takes our order, then drops the food off when it was done and brought us our check. Never gave us ice water or asked anything, although all the other tables had ice water and he kept bringing them sauces or whatever they asked for. I finally got up and got my own water from the dispenser from the lobby.    My final bill had $100 pet fee, even though I specified at check-in he was a service animal, and would have happily supplied his airline paperwork that they even printed for me.    I would recommend staying elsewhere and wouldn’t stay here again.

Dear Sarah,    Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. While we’re glad to hear that you found the room clean and comfortable, we’re truly sorry to learn about the service concerns you experienced during your stay. That is certainly not the level of attentiveness or consistency we strive to provide, particularly in our food and beverage outlets.    Your comments regarding poolside and breakfast service have been shared with our management team so we can address the gaps you described and ensure better communication and follow-through moving forward. We also regret the confusion surrounding the pet fee on your final bill. Service animals are, of course, accommodated in accordance with policy, and we understand how frustrating it must have been to see that charge appear.    We appreciate you bringing these matters to our attention and hope you might consider giving us another opportunity to provide a more seamless and positive experience in the future.    Warm regards,

We had a lovely stay, Guillermo was especially great.    For those considering staying here:  Be sure to check your bill/credit card after your stay. They gave me an incomplete receipt at check out and added on 2 more amenity tax charges that should have been included in the original, plus a bogus mini bar charge. We also found another $21 charged to our card a few days after our stay, I asked for the receipt for it and they could not produce it. I requested a receipt for it twice, the manager ghosted me. It’s not much, I recognize that, but not a great look for this nice of a hotel to have this lax of accounting and to not be able to send me the receipt and ghosting me are more the issue here. Just, beware.    And bring bug spray and ointment. There was a mosquito infestation that seemed local to the Avalon. During our stay at the Ingleside we went over to the Avalon a few times and got eaten alive - our waiter at ChiChi was clapping to kill them while she took our order. I’m pretty sure the Saturday morning Pilates was held in their nest on the presidio courtyard, I was covered in bug bites.

Dear Guest,    Thank you for taking the time to share your detailed feedback and for recognizing Guillermo’s service — we’re delighted to hear he made a great impression during your visit.    We’re very sorry to learn of the billing confusion you experienced after your stay. Transparency and accuracy are extremely important to us, and we understand how frustrating it can be when a charge appears unclear. Our accounting and front office teams have reviewed your comments to ensure receipts and billing records are always communicated promptly and completely moving forward.    We also appreciate your note regarding mosquitos at Avalon. While the desert climate can make this an occasional challenge, our maintenance team continues to work closely with our pest control partners to minimize any presence and keep our outdoor areas as comfortable as possible.    We truly value your feedback and hope you’ll consider giving us another opportunity to provide the seamless, restorative experience our guests know us for.    Warm regards,  Cody Elliott  Rooms & Wellness Manager

Dear Jason,    Thank you for taking the time to share your rating and feedback. We’re glad to know aspects of your stay met expectations, though we understand there may have been opportunities to make your experience even better. Your comments are appreciated as we continuously strive to enhance our service and amenities.    We hope to welcome you back for an even more enjoyable visit in the future.    Warm regards,  Cody Elliott  Rooms & Wellness Manager
L'Horizon Resort & Spa Palm Springs, CA
Lots of chatter about how quiet it is, but we disagree. Road noise could also hear our neighbors next door at Times great restaurant.

The front office staff was always so friendly. They gave great recommendations and provided ice. The dog bed and water and food bowls that our dog used was so awesome, our dog loved it. The cleaning staff was also very helpful with new glasses. Great experience!

This hotel is a real Palm Springs gem and an important part of PS history. Great ambiance, food, drinks. Best deviled eggs I’ve ever had. Live music every night. Room was big and comfy. Quiet with easy access to downtown. Big shower. Nice private patio. Pool. If you’re looking for an ultramodern hotel, this is not your place. If you’re looking to experience Palm Springs history and vibe this is your place

This place is incredibly charming especially if you enjoy a bit of old glamorous history. Adults only, dogs welcome, cute patios. Loved Melvyns and the pool. Just a great, quiet stay in walking distance to restaurants and shops.

We loved this peaceful getaway! It’s a great cozy set of bungalows and we enjoyed every minute of our two day stay. We especially loved the hot tub and the fire pit. The staff was really friendly and welcoming and offered terrific local tips. They are short staffed in the mornings so be prepared for a wait for breakfast.

This is a classic property with a relaxed but elegant vibe. We loved that it is smaller and felt private. The staff was gracious, the restaurant was great, and the pool was a nice amenity during a very warm stay in May. We'd definitely stay here again.

New restaurant management is terrible  - New Year's Eve celebration, so fun in past years, was a disaster. Multiple complaints from everyone at the table, but absolutely no response from management. Difficult to believe a Palm Springs institution has fallen so far.
It’s a great historic property but breakfast service on the veranda could be improved

The stay could have been great, but the walls are very thin. Could hear restaurant adjacent to hotel cleaning outside  late into the night. No one at the front desk to assist with noise. Could hear the guest above the room snoring. Beautiful place, but sadly disappointed.

We stayed here because it is part of the Chase “edit” hotels program. As part of the benefits we were upgraded to a deluxe room. The room was small and clean for the most part, the bed comfortable.    The food and beverage service here, was the worst I’ve experienced in a while. 3pm at the pool and there were only about 10 other people. The blonde employee would come and leave, cleaning the area, saying she would be right back to take our order. After 30 minutes we gave up, and I had wine delivered from BevMo.    The next morning at breakfast, we were simply ignored for 15 minutes. Watching the server come and go getting things for the other tables. He finally comes over and takes our order, then drops the food off when it was done and brought us our check. Never gave us ice water or asked anything, although all the other tables had ice water and he kept bringing them sauces or whatever they asked for. I finally got up and got my own water from the dispenser from the lobby.    My final bill had $100 pet fee, even though I specified at check-in he was a service animal, and would have happily supplied his airline service animal paperwork that they even printed for me.    I would recommend staying elsewhere and wouldn’t stay here again.

We stayed here because it is part of the Chase “edit” hotels program. As part of the benefits we were upgraded to a deluxe room. The room was small and clean for the most part, the bed comfortable.     The food and beverage service here, was the worst I’ve experienced in a while. 3pm at the pool and there were only about 10 other people. The blonde employee would come and leave, cleaning the area, saying she would be right back to take our order. After 30 minutes we gave up, and I had wine delivered from BevMo.    The next morning at breakfast, we were simply ignored for 15 minutes. Watching the server come and go getting things for the other tables. He finally comes over and takes our order, then drops the food off when it was done and brought us our check. Never gave us ice water or asked anything, although all the other tables had ice water and he kept bringing them sauces or whatever they asked for. I finally got up and got my own water from the dispenser from the lobby.    My final bill had $100 pet fee, even though I specified at check-in he was a service animal, and would have happily supplied his airline paperwork that they even printed for me.    I would recommend staying elsewhere and wouldn’t stay here again.

We visited Melvyn’s during a busy wedding weekend in Palm Springs, and even with the restaurant packed, Sarah was exceptional. She was our bartender and waitress, and despite juggling a full crowd, she was incredibly attentive, warm, and on top of every detail.    Our drinks were perfectly made, her recommendations were spot-on, and she made sure our entire meal felt special. She went above and beyond to check on us, keep everything moving smoothly, and make sure we never felt rushed or overlooked.    Sarah turned an already fun night into a truly memorable one. If you’re visiting Melvyn’s, ask for her, she’s fantastic.

We only had a short time in Palm Springs so we wanted to stay in a place that reflected the true character of the area. The Ingleside Estate is like a hidden gem right in town. The property is beautiful and the staff is so friendly and helpful After a couple of hours exploring the mid-century neighborhood nearby on the inn's bikes, we hopped in the hot tub, swam in the pool and sat by the fire pit. The grounds are immaculate. The room itself is so lovely--they renovated but kept the charming details. The bathroom is new and very stylish. After the shower, I donned the robe and slippers and chilled out before heading out to dinner at Workshop. We also had the cutest private patio where we had our coffee the next morning. We'll be back!
Old school Spanish bungalows are lovely.  The walls and grounds, which date from the 1930’s, are visibly deteriorating.  Melvyn’s, the grand restaurant on site, is always pricey, but marvelous.

The room was amazing- huge and beautiful! The bathroom was on the snug side and we did have a leaky tap but a flannel sorted it out for a peaceful sleep.  The pool was fabulous- very relaxing.    Only issue is they need more staff- you will wait for breakfast/ pool service etc.

We had a lovely stay, Guillermo was especially great.    For those considering staying here:  Be sure to check your bill/credit card after your stay. They gave me an incomplete receipt at check out and added on 2 more amenity tax charges that should have been included in the original, plus a bogus mini bar charge. We also found another $21 charged to our card a few days after our stay, I asked for the receipt for it and they could not produce it. I requested a receipt for it twice, the manager ghosted me. It’s not much, I recognize that, but not a great look for this nice of a hotel to have this lax of accounting and to not be able to send me the receipt and ghosting me are more the issue here. Just, beware.    And bring bug spray and ointment. There was a mosquito infestation that seemed local to the Avalon. During our stay at the Ingleside we went over to the Avalon a few times and got eaten alive - our waiter at ChiChi was clapping to kill them while she took our order. I’m pretty sure the Saturday morning Pilates was held in their nest on the presidio courtyard, I was covered in bug bites.

Good: Elegant, historic but not dated, quiet & unfussy vibe, friendly and helpful staff, comfortable and spacious room, private terrace with gorgeous Mountain View.    Bad: There was no signage in the front/main driveway to identify Reception (only in the rear parking area) so we (and we weren’t the only ones) made a loop or two of the property before guessing correctly that the first set of doors was indeed for the hotel (as Melvyn’s restaurant is attached to the main hotel building that was not obvious). Check-in itself was easy and staff were friendly and eager to assist.
Wonderful old Hollywood.  Melvyn's is the best for dinner.  Great piano bar.  Staff very accommodating and made you welcome at both the Inn and at the restaurant.
I felt like I was in a Taylor swift music video the entire time. Whimsical, magical, romantic oasis. Staff and food and drinks were exceptional ❤️❤️❤️
As previously noted, Shelly was exceptional!
Melvyns is now in my top five ever for food and service!
6 oz steak was not 6 oz. More like 4
Service and food quality were excellent.

WISH THE STEAK DIANE WAS THE TRADITIONAL WAY WITH MEDALLIONS OF TENDERLOIN INSTEAD OF THE FIET. THE FILET WAS QUITE CHEWY, MAYBE NOT THE BEST GRADE. ALSO THE SAUCE WAS RATHER BLAND. OUR SERVER WAS VERY NICE
Love Melvyn’s, I’ve been going since I was a child with my parents. It’s so nostalgic for me.
Fun and lively bar with great staff

For the first day breakfast service was exceedingly slow, perhaps because there was a staff shortage, all though we were not informed if that was the case.  Other days we let the server know we had to be somewhere at a certain time and they were very helpful in accommodating us.

We were very disappointed with our meal at Melvyn's. We had the chicken pot pie and the scallops, both of which we found very disappointing. They didn't taste good. The drinks - an old fashioned and a French martini - also were not great. The bread, however, was very good. Our meal at Melvyn's was in sharp contrast to all the other meals we had in Palm Springs at other restaurants - such as Rooster & The Pig and Bar Cecil - which were fresh, innovative, flavorful and delicious. We have the impression that Melvyn's is tired and riding on its reputation. Melvyn's was our only negative experience of Ingleside Estate.

Professional service and execution. Delightful dining experience. I’m splitting hairs, but I would like to see more flexibility with the menu I.e: they were sold out of carrots and I inquired about just getting a side of sautéed spinach as I don’t want creamed and I was told they don’t do that. So, having one vegetable that’s not creamed or fried on the menu isn’t really guest forward. Have the classic starchy, creamy steakhouse sides but also offer à la carte veggies - the carrots sold out so ppl clearly want to eat farmers market vegetables w their heavier meat counterparts.
Restaurant seems understaffed.  We really enjoyed our dinner and our servers were very friendly and helpful (Orlando and Manny), but the service was very slow.

Table service could have been better.  Server was not very attentive.  We sat with empty dinner plates after eating, for example, for quite a while before plates were removed and we were offered dessert.  Birthday was barely recognized.  .
The cold brew was terrible. Watered down would be a compliment.

Food was not great, although nice atmosphere.  It was also my husbands birthday - which the host at the front confirmed, but then they never did anything for it or even recognized it at the table (candle on dessert, etc)
My breakfast at Ingleside was outstanding.  I had two eggs over easy with toast and bacon, and it was a dream. Bacon, eggs and toast were perfectly cooked.
Need more options, and smaller steak portions.  The Branzino on the other hand was not enough for 2 people.  Kinda pricey.
See previous note. We love Brandon!
Took a long time and not all items were brought up. Took over two hours to complete our order…
We miss the old menu, which had many breakfast choices.
We would have liked to have enjoyed our dinner on the beautiful patio instead of the room because we had our dog.
I will be dreaming of that burrito!
Guest came to check in but he had no reservation. Had to walk Guest in for 1 Nt.
Front Office - Room Not Ready- Not Available Upon Arrival

Mrs. Biggerstaff reported calling and speaking with the staff to extend their arrival date. When they arrived there had been no changes made to their reservaiton and had to book a different room type.
Guest came to check in but he had no reservation. Had to walk Guest in for 1 Nt.
At departure Ms. Gravett reported the towels in her room containing pests. She advised she left them outside but did not have towels to use
Guest was having issues with the nosie from the kitchen fans at Melvyn's as well as landscapers working at 10am around the pool. Guest was not happy that is was loud when trying to relax.
Front Office - Room Key Not Working

After check in guest was upset that a second key to the room was not given and that the front desk did not currently have a second key to give to them and felt this was a security risk and wanted to speak to a manager about getting a second key.
Front Office - Room - Not as Expected
Guest was not happyt about getting an ECI but also was liking the property. Advised that they were more than happy to explore APS. Guet decided to transfer over to APS

On arrival, we informed Carah Fink of our check-in time and explained that her children would be able to enjoy the property during that period, as the full property buyout would not begin until 4:00 PM and other hotel guests outside of her wedding block were still departing.
Amenity use outside of the wedding block time frame.

Guest booked a PK through a 3rd party (City Bank) which was booked into an ADA room.
Front Office - Room - Price / Value Complaint

Mr. Leitersdorf reported encountering two separate cockroaches in the bathroom on two occasions approximately one hour apart. The guest expressed dissatisfaction and noted that, given the high price point of their stay, this issue was especially concerning.
Front Office - Room - Not as Expected

came to check in and when mentioned what room she was she started to freak out stating that she couldn't stay in the room due to noise from Melvyn's. Showed them 03 and didn't like either due to being near the road. Did let them to look at 30 and let me know. Spoke to   Candice   due to the expressing that they had booked under a rate with F&B credit. Advised the guest I would speak to a manager. Per ?  Candice  , we will be adding a $25 F&B credit per Day
